text:
BOTTOM LINE — Tech chased, energy dumped, breadth quietly deteriorated. VIX slipped to 16.3 and a tech-led risk-on move off Dell’s >30% earnings spike pulled indices higher even as only 47.1% of names rose and uvol/dvol sat at 0.79, showing weak internal sponsorship. XLK’s 2.23% surge, software and cloud baskets ripping, and oil’s 4.36% slide on US‑Iran peace hopes framed a classic growth-chase day with cyclicals favored over defensives despite narrow leadership from specific software names.

INDICES — VIX 16.3, down vs its 20-day 17.4, signaled contained equity vol with MOVE at 70.9 confirming calmer rates. QQQ +0.37%, SPY +0.25%, DIA +0.74%, IWM -0.55% put DIA > QQQ > SPY > IWM, with STYLE reading LARGE > SMALL by 0.92pp. GLD’s -1.33% and USO’s -4.36% made crude’s dump the standout cross-asset move, while SPY remained above its cited 20-day 5.26% trend profile so no 200-day CTA sell trigger fired.

BREADTH — Percent above SMA20/50/200 sat at 57.2%, 60.1%, and 66.8%, with 98 advancers vs 110 decliners, 47.1% up, and uvol/dvol of 0.79 painting “index strong, internals weak.” Mag7 median -1.23% versus ex‑Mag7 -0.05% with narrow_leadership=True plus SPY +0.25% vs universe median -0.14% showed mega-cap underperformance but benchmark outperformance, confirming a thin tape not driven by the usual giants.

VOLUME — SPY traded at 1.1x average volume while QQQ sat near the 1.03x index cluster, indicating modest attention rather than a blowout tape. XLK at 1.2x, XLRE and XLV at 1.4x, alongside XHE 5.1x and PPH 4.8x, showed flows concentrating in tech and healthcare subsectors, with price up + volume up in XLK signaling accumulation and price down + volume up in XLV/XHE/PPH signaling distribution.

SECTORS — XLK Technology +2.23%, XLF Financials +0.60%, XLI Industrials -0.39%, XLB Materials -0.41%, XLU Utilities -0.47%, XLC Comms -0.84%, XLV Healthcare -0.93%, XLRE Real Estate -0.95%, XLY Discretionary -0.97%, XLE Energy -1.16%, XLP Staples -1.80%. Top industries were WCLD +7.96% and CIBR +6.41%, while XRT -1.89% and MSOS -3.91% anchored the downside.

ROTATION — Money-in clearly favored software and cloud (WCLD +7.96%, IGV +6.25%) plus broader tech (XLK +2.23%), while money rotated out of defensives like XLP -1.80% and XLU -0.47%, in line with defensives_minus_cyclicals at -0.54pp for a cyclical lean. Leadership clustered in tech with The Software 7 +9.58% and The Cloud 7 +4.40% versus The New Staples 7 -1.30% and The Old Staples 7 -1.68%, so label this “software catching up.”

THE 7s — Top baskets: Software +9.58%, Cloud +4.40%, Old Metals +1.63%, Bank +1.21%, Industrial +0.36%, with NOW +14% pacing Software and IBM +12.71% driving Cloud while weaker members lagged. Bottom baskets: New Staples -1.30%, Energy -0.68%, Insurance -0.63%, Buildout -0.61%, Health -1.06%, with XOM-type energy components dragging Energy and defensive staples constituents weighing on New Staples.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC sits on Jun 17 with CME probabilities at hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%, and a stance tally of 6 hawks, 5 neutrals, 4 doves signaling a mildly hawkish-leaning committee. USD liquidity stress printed 51/100 “Elevated,” with CP−Tbill at the 86th percentile and Reserve Scarcity at the 77th percentile, while TED at 0.09% and ON‑RRP at $1.9B showed no acute funding panic.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 1.19 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.35 with positive skew (+3.3% and +8.0%) showed solid index put demand and put premia, i.e., protection chasing into strength. With VIX 16.3 below its 20-day 17.4 and GEX flip levels at 750 for SPY and 722 for QQQ above spot, dealers remain long gamma, dampening intraday swings unless spot trades up toward those strikes.

CROSS-ASSET — USO -4.36% alongside OVX 60.0 and HY OAS at 2.72% in the 5th percentile highlighted a risk-on equity day funded partly by collapsing oil rather than credit stress. GLD -1.33% and SLV -3.18% falling while VIXY -2.07% and MOVE 70.9 eased underscored that safe havens were being unwound as AI and software narratives dominated flows.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 61.6/100 sat in “Greed” territory. Top movers across groups were IBM +12.71% in The Cloud 7, WDAY +11.57% in software growth, and SOCL +1.57% in social-media industries, with vol spikes in IBM at 3.5x and SOCL at 3.9x indicating accumulation on AI and SaaS earnings narratives.

WATCH — IBM +12.71% deserves focus given 3.5x volume, Cloud 7 membership, and its role as a legacy AI beneficiary tied to Dell’s print. WDAY +11.57% merits attention as a high-growth software name with strong earnings momentum feeding The Software 7’s +9.58% surge. XLP -1.80% with weakening 20d -1.50% and sector outflows makes staples an important barometer for any reversal from the current cyclical, software-led chase.
